name: HAB-Hardware-Grounded-Arduino-Benchmark
rai:dataCollection: Arduino projects were collected from publicly accessible repositories and maker community platforms (e.g., Arduino Project Hub, Instructables, GitHub). Projects were programmatically identified, then manually reviewed for hardware diversity, code completeness, and license compatibility.
rai:dataUseCases: Primary use: benchmarking Large Language Models (LLMs) on hardware-aware Arduino code generation tasks. Secondary uses include studying prompt engineering for embedded systems, evaluating hardware-software co-design reasoning, and developing automated code-correctness metrics for microcontroller applications.
rai:dataLimitations: The dataset is limited to Arduino-compatible microcontroller boards and English-language project descriptions. Projects are sourced from specific online platforms, which may introduce selection bias toward hobbyist and educational use cases. Hardware component coverage is influenced by the popularity of components in the source communities. Projects requiring proprietary libraries or closed-source hardware are excluded.
rai:dataPreprocessingProtocol: Raw project data was extracted from source platforms, deduplicated, and normalized into a uniform JSONL schema. Hardware components were mapped to a curated hardware dictionary. Library dependencies were resolved against the Arduino Library Registry. Projects were split into Train/Validation/Test sets with stratification by difficulty level and board family. A privacy audit was performed to remove any PII or user-specific file paths.
rai:annotatorDemographics: Annotations (hardware component labels, pin assignments, difficulty ratings, library mappings) were produced by the dataset authors, who are domain experts in embedded systems and Arduino development. No crowd-sourced annotators were used.
rai:dataBiases: The dataset reflects a bias toward English-language projects and platforms popular in hobbyist and educational communities (e.g., Arduino Project Hub, Instructables, GitHub). Component and library coverage skews toward widely-used, low-cost sensors and actuators. Professional or industrial Arduino applications are underrepresented. Projects from non-English-speaking communities and less popular hardware are likely underrepresented.
rai:dataSocialImpact: HAB is intended to advance research in hardware-aware code generation, enabling LLMs to produce safer and more physically-grounded embedded systems code. Potential positive impacts include accelerating prototyping for engineers and educators, and lowering the barrier for hardware programming. Potential risks include over-reliance on generated code in safety-critical hardware contexts; users should always validate generated code against hardware datasheets before deployment.

prov:wasGeneratedBy.name: Data Collection
prov:wasGeneratedBy.description: Web scraping of public Arduino projects from Arduino Project Hub, Instructables, and GitHub. Projects were programmatically identified using keyword and category filters, then downloaded for review.

prov:wasAssociatedWith.name: Dataset authors (domain experts in embedded systems)

prov:wasGeneratedBy.name: Preprocessing and Filtering
prov:wasGeneratedBy.description: Deduplication, schema normalization to JSONL format, license compatibility filtering, privacy audit to remove PII and user-specific file paths, and stratified Train/Validation/Test splitting by difficulty and board family.

prov:wasAssociatedWith.name: Dataset authors with automated Python pipeline

prov:wasGeneratedBy.name: Annotation
prov:wasGeneratedBy.description: Manual annotation of hardware component labels, pin assignments, difficulty ratings (Low/Medium/High), and required library mappings. All annotations performed by dataset authors with domain expertise in Arduino and embedded systems.

prov:wasAssociatedWith.name: Dataset authors (no crowdsourcing or synthetic agents used)
